names.thesis: Novelis' FY2026 10-K shows net income cratering 98% YoY to $15M (from $683M) and Adjusted EBITDA down 9% to $1,645M, driven by the Oswego plant fires, weaker recycled-metal benefit, and higher net tariffs, with adjusted free cash flow swinging to a $2.4B outflow. As a fabricator (Hindalco subsidiary) exposed to LME aluminum "metal price lag" and regional premium volatility rather than upside commodity beta, this confirms the packaging/fabricator short thesis on tariff and cost pass-through drag.
catalysts: Bay Minette plant ramp-up completion
Recovery from Oswego fire disruption
Tariff/regional premium normalization
risks: Further tariff escalation compressing margins
Metal price lag timing mismatches
Parent Hindalco capital allocation priorities

evidence.note: Net income down 98% to $15M; Adjusted EBITDA down 9% to $1,645M; adjusted FCF outflow of $2.4B; drivers: Oswego fires, lower recycled inputs, higher net tariffs.
FY2026 net sales $18.4B; confirms Hindalco/Aditya Birla ownership structure.

names.thesis: No operating or steel-price commentary surfaced in the window; instead, Grupo Simec has twice disclosed inability to timely file its FY2025 Form 20-F, triggering NYSE non-compliance risk, with the company citing ongoing internal-control and disclosure procedure reviews. This governance/filing delinquency is a caution flag that sits on top of (not a repudiation of) the broader steel-long thesis, but it lowers near-term conviction until financials are filed and reviewed.
catalysts: Filing of delayed Form 20-F for FY2025
Resolution of NYSE listing compliance discussions
risks: Prolonged filing delay risking NYSE delisting action
Unknown nature of the disclosure/internal-control issues being reviewed
Opacity into actual FY2025/Q1 2026 steel fundamentals until 20-F is filed

evidence.note: Company not in compliance with SEC reporting requirements and NYSE listing standards after failing to file Form 20-F for FY2025; in discussions with NYSE.
Second notice that Form 20-F would not be filed within the extended Rule 12b-25 period; delay attributed to administrative steps.

names.thesis: First Quantum's newsflow reinforces the copper-long thesis: RBC reiterated Outperform (C$45 PT) and National Bank Outperform ($47 PT) on the back of an updated La Granja JV technical report with Rio Tinto (world's second-largest greenfield copper resource, 23Mt contained Cu measured & indicated), while 2026 copper production guidance at Guelb Moghrein was raised alongside continued advancement of the Taca Taca project. Q2 2026 results land July 28-29, a near-term catalyst.
catalysts: Taca Taca approvals expected in 2026
La Granja JV de-risking with Rio Tinto
risks: Zambia/Panama operational and political risk
Gold production guidance cut at Guelb Moghrein (deferred to 2027) signals execution slippage
Broader copper price volatility on tariff/Fed policy uncertainty

evidence.note: RBC and National Bank both reiterate bullish ratings citing La Granja JV technical report (23Mt contained copper in measured & indicated resources).

evidence.source: Model ML - Slides (RBC Global Mining Conference)
evidence.note: 2026 copper production guidance raised to 7kt (from 1.5kt) at Guelb Moghrein; gold guidance cut and deferred to 2027.
Q2 2026 results scheduled for release July 28, 2026.

names.thesis: Beijing Shougang's FY2025 net profit doubled YoY (+107.68% to RMB996M) even as revenue slipped 5.11%, but Q1 2026 shows deterioration -- revenue down 5.65% and net profit down 47.17% YoY -- tempering near-term momentum. The structural long case rests on electrical-steel capacity expansion for EVs (550kt high-grade non-oriented electrical steel, plus new oriented silicon steel lines), which is a multi-year volume/mix tailwind independent of the weak Q1 print.
catalysts: Ramp of new EV-grade electrical steel capacity
High-end product mix shift (targeting >70% of electrical steel output)
Analyst day / CITIC Securities coverage follow-through
risks: Q1 2026 profit down 47% YoY signals near-term margin pressure
Chinese steel demand/pricing cyclicality
Overcapacity risk in domestic steel sector

analyst.Coverage: 3 institutions in past 90 days: 1 buy, 2 overweight

evidence.note: FY2025 revenue RMB102.9B (-5.11% YoY), net profit RMB996M (+107.68% YoY).

evidence.source: Flush Financial Services Network (analyst meeting w/ CITIC Securities)
evidence.note: Q1 2026 revenue RMB25.08B (-5.65% YoY), net profit RMB174M (-47.17% YoY); gross margin 4.64%.
EV electrical steel dedicated line at 550,000t capacity; high-end products ~64% of electrical steel output, targeting >70% by 2025.

names.thesis: Thyssenkrupp confirmed its FY25/26 adjusted EBIT guidance (EUR500-900M) despite trimming the sales forecast slightly (to -3% to 0%) on Steel Europe product mix and restructuring provisions. BofA reiterated Buy, citing the planned tk accelis (Materials Services) spinoff by end-2026 as a value-unlock catalyst and flagging upside potential for European steel prices from the newly announced Tariff Rate Quota (TRQ) trade protection -- a direct positive for the steel-long thesis.
catalysts: tk accelis (Materials Services) spinoff listing by end-2026
Aug 7, 2026 EGM approval vote for the spinoff
EU TRQ tariff protection lifting European steel prices
risks: Steel Europe restructuring provisions driving continued net losses (EUR-400M to -800M forecast)
Sales forecast already cut once this fiscal year
Execution risk on multi-part conglomerate breakup

evidence.note: BofA maintains Buy (EUR15 PT), values Materials Services spinoff at ~EUR3.5B and flags upside from EU TRQ tariff protection for steel prices.
FY26 adjusted EBIT guidance maintained at EUR500-900M; sales forecast cut to -3%/0% (from -2%/+1%) on Steel Europe mix and Decarbon Technologies revenue timing.

names.thesis: Despite CFRA raising 2026/2027 EPS estimates and Barrick posting a 195% YoY surge in Q1 FCF to $1.21B on maintained production guidance (2.90-3.25Moz gold, 190-220kt copper), spot gold has fallen roughly 26% from its January 2026 peak above $5,500/oz to the $4,100-4,150 range by early July -- its steepest multi-quarter decline since 2013 -- pressuring Barrick shares even as fundamentals remain solid. This confirms the short-medium thesis: strong operating leverage cuts both ways, and price momentum has decisively broken down.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results Aug 10, 2026
Planned year-end North American IPO
Lumwana expansion (Q1 2028 first production) and Fourmile Tier-One potential
risks: Further gold price downside on Fed policy/rate path
Geopolitical de-escalation (Iran) removing safe-haven bid
High operating leverage amplifies downside to earnings if price weakness persists

analyst.CFRA: Buy, $56 12-month PT (2026 EPS raised to $4.04, 2027 to $4.63)

evidence.note: PT $56; Q1 FCF +195% YoY to $1.21B; production guidance maintained; $2.4B net cash.
Gold down ~26% from Jan 2026 peak (>$5,500/oz) to $4,100-4,150/oz range; Barrick shares fell 1.8% pre-market on the decline.

names.thesis: Coverage in the window was sparse and mostly index/screener-derived rather than earnings-specific. African Rainbow Minerals remains a genuinely diversified miner (iron ore/manganese via ARM Ferrous ZAR17.8B, PGM via Two Rivers ZAR8.0B and Modikwa ZAR3.7B, plus coal and gold), which was flagged as a South African export name with tariff sensitivity -- consistent with treating it as a diversified, lower-conviction leg of the gold/PGM short-medium bucket rather than a standalone catalyst-driven short.
catalysts: Potential softening of US tariffs on South African exports
Manganese/PGM segment price moves flowing through diversified mix
risks: US tariff policy on South African exports
Multi-commodity exposure dilutes any single-thesis edge (gold, PGM, manganese, iron ore, coal all moving pieces)
No FY2026 guidance or earnings specifics surfaced in this window

evidence.note: Flagged as a South African export-dependent, tariff-sensitive diversified miner (iron ore, manganese, PGM, nickel, coal, chrome, gold); revenue breakdown by segment given.

names.thesis: Manganese spot prices spiked toward US$5.16/dmtu CIF (Fastmarkets semi-carbonate index) on Middle East supply disruption, lifting Tshipi's annualized EBITDA run-rate above A$120M and supporting Jupiter's debt-free, high-payout structure (historically ~84% of share price returned via dividends). However, underlying earnings have declined ~10% annually over five years and recent insider selling was flagged, while a speculative Exxaro takeover premium discussion remains forum-sourced and unconfirmed -- consistent with a neutral stance where near-term price tailwinds are offset by weak longer-run earnings trend and unclear M&A catalyst.
catalysts: Sustained manganese price strength feeding Tshipi JV cash flow
Speculative Exxaro consolidation of the Tshipi structure
Continued high dividend payout given zero corporate debt
risks: Manganese price spike partly tied to transient Middle East supply disruption, could fade
5-year earnings decline trend (-10% annually)
Recent insider selling
Takeover speculation unconfirmed/forum-sourced

evidence.note: Earnings A$9.45M, FCF positive A$4.47M, no debt, but earnings declined 10%/yr over 5 years; notable recent insider selling; P/E 12.8x vs market 17.1x.

Manganese semi-carbonate index surged toward US$5.16/dmtu CIF on Middle East disruption; Tshipi annualized EBITDA run-rate >A$120M; speculative Exxaro takeover premium analysis (unconfirmed, forum source).

names.thesis: Centerra's operating results run counter to a bearish stance: Q1 2026 revenue jumped 62% YoY to $484.7M, net earnings +160% to $79.4M, and free cash flow reached $49M on strong Mount Milligan and Öksüt grades, with L1 Capital initiating a new $125M position. This is a diversified gold/copper/moly (Thompson Creek) producer with a self-funded growth pipeline (Kemess, Thompson Creek, Goldfield); no explicit molybdenum price commentary surfaced, but the strength of the print argues for tempering conviction on the short leg for this specific name within the gold-bucket short-medium thesis.
catalysts: Q2 2026 results Jul 28-29, 2026
Thompson Creek (molybdenum) project advancement
Continued institutional accumulation (L1 Capital and peers)
risks: Still gold-price-linked despite operational strength; a broader gold selloff (as seen at Barrick) would compress margins
Execution risk across multiple growth projects simultaneously
Molybdenum price exposure specifics not evidenced in this window

evidence.note: Q1 2026 revenue +62% YoY to $484.7M, net earnings +160% to $79.4M, FCF $49M, cash $543.5M; new $125M L1 Capital position.
Q2 2026 results scheduled for release July 28, 2026, with call July 29.

names.thesis: Nornickel itself forecasts a global nickel market surplus of ~20,000 tonnes in 2026 and ~55,000 tonnes in 2027, consistent with the neutral-to-bearish nickel view, while LME nickel fell to $17,230/ton in the window. The board recommended skipping the 2025 dividend to preserve financial stability amid 'elevated macroeconomic instability,' and though not under direct sanctions, Western measures are pushing buyers away from Russian metal and forcing sales redirection to Asia. CRITICAL: GMKN.ME trades on MOEX (Moscow Exchange) and is not tradable through IBKR given Russia sanctions exposure -- this is a research-only/non-actionable name regardless of the fundamental view.
catalysts: Potential 2026 dividend resumption per CEO commentary
Copper concentrate deficit / zero benchmark TCs for 2026 (positive for copper segment)
risks: NOT tradable via IBKR (sanctioned/foreign-exchange listing)
IBKR/Western brokerage tradability -- MOEX-listed, effectively inaccessible to US/EU investors under sanctions regime
Nickel market surplus forecast by the company itself
Further Western sanctions escalation on Russian metals trade

evidence.note: Nornickel forecasts global nickel surplus of ~20,000t in 2026 and ~55,000t in 2027; LME nickel fell to $17,230/ton.
Board recommends no 2025 dividend citing macro instability; EBITDA +9% to $5.7B; not under direct sanctions but Western measures reduce Western buyer appetite for Russian metal.
Nornickel forecasts copper concentrate deficit and zero benchmark treatment charges for 2026.

names.thesis: Southern Palladium remains a pre-production developer with no earnings or PGM sales yet; the latest news is purely metallurgical test-work progress at Bengwenyama (PGM recovery improved to 87.6%, platinum-weighted metal mix of 44.8% Pt/34.4% Pd supporting a stronger basket price assumption), with the definitive feasibility study not due until Q4 2026. This confirms the high-risk framing of the PGM short thesis for this specific name: it carries full development/financing/permitting execution risk with zero current cash flow, independent of near-term PGM price direction.
catalysts: DFS completion due Q4 2026
Continued positive metallurgical test-work de-risking the project
Potential strategic partner or offtake announcement
risks: No revenue or production until well after DFS/financing/construction
PGM basket price assumptions embedded in DFS economics are speculative
Financing/dilution risk typical of ASX-listed developers

evidence.note: DFS test work shows PGM recovery up to 87.6% (from 85.3% PFS), platinum-weighted metal distribution (44.8% Pt/34.4% Pd), full DFS due Q4 2026; still pre-production.

names.thesis: Lynas struck a capital-light downstream partnership with JS Link (AU$50M for equity stake), backing a 3,000tpa NdFeB magnet plant in Malaysia and an exclusive rare-earth supply deal through January 2038; combined with its South Korean magnet-plant offtake, this creates a new ~1,330 tonne/year NdPr demand channel alongside its existing JARE agreement and US DoD letter of intent. Jefferies maintained Buy (AU$22 PT), explicitly citing support for a non-China rare-earth supply chain -- directly reinforcing the long thesis around Lynas as a key non-China producer with equities lagging fundamentals.
catalysts: Q4 FY26 quarterly results and analyst briefing, Jul 22, 2026
JS Link magnet plant construction progress in Malaysia and South Korea
Continued NdPr price strength (+74% YoY per backdrop)
risks: AU$50M capital commitment to JS Link equity stake
Execution risk on new downstream magnet-plant partnerships
China rare-earth policy responses to Western supply-chain diversification

evidence.note: Jefferies Buy, AU$22 PT; JS Link deal creates ~1,330t/yr NdPr demand channel; supports non-China rare earth supply chain thesis.
AU$50M investment in JS Link; 3,000tpa magnet factory in Kuantan, Malaysia; exclusive supply deal to Jan 2038.
Q4 FY26 quarterly results and analyst/shareholder briefing scheduled for July 22, 2026.

names.thesis: Empire Metals finalized an integrated metallurgical flowsheet at its Pitfield titanium project in Western Australia, achieving 98% titanium extraction and a 99% TiO2 pigment product in bench-scale testwork, with piloting starting Q3 2026 and feasibility studies in Q4. This is purely development-stage progress with an updated mineral resource estimate due July/August 2026 -- consistent with a neutral stance, as there is no production, revenue, or titanium price exposure yet, though technical de-risking is proceeding on schedule.
catalysts: Updated Mineral Resource Estimate (Jul/Aug 2026)
Continuous metallurgical piloting starting Q3 2026
Feasibility studies in Q4 2026
risks: Pre-revenue explorer with no near-term cash flow
Piloting/feasibility results could still reveal cost or scale-up challenges
Reference industry production costs ($1,300-3,300/t for rutile pigment routes) frame a wide potential cost outcome

evidence.note: Integrated metallurgical flowsheet finalized: 99% TiO2 pigment achievable, 98% titanium extraction, high-grade alumina co-product; industry rutile pigment costs $1,300-3,300/t.
Continuous metallurgical piloting to start Q3 2026 to validate design criteria ahead of feasibility studies.

names.thesis: Malaysia Smelting delivered a record quarter: Q1 FY26 net profit surged more than fivefold YoY to RM42.93M on revenue +23.6% to RM457.04M, driven by tin prices up 32% YTD to ~US$53,900/tonne on the LME -- closely matching the backdrop's $53,450 reference level -- and the board declared both a final and special dividend. Public Investment Bank's outlook remains positive on continued supply deficit (Myanmar, Indonesia disruptions) keeping prices elevated, directly confirming the tin-long thesis.
catalysts: Continued tin supply deficit from Myanmar/Indonesia disruptions
MSC scaling mining investment to capture higher volumes at elevated prices
Potential rare-earth optionality given MSC's mining infrastructure
risks: Tin price pullback if Myanmar/Indonesia supply normalizes faster than expected
Margin sensitivity to LME price swings given smelting cost structure

analyst.Public Investment Bank: Positive outlook on tin market, no explicit PT cited in evidence

evidence.note: Q1 FY26 net profit up ~5.5x YoY to RM42.93M; tin prices +32% YTD to ~US$53,900/tonne LME.
Tin prices US$49,000-56,000/tonne LME as of May 2026; supply concerns in Myanmar/Indonesia plus energy/digital transition demand sustaining elevated prices.
Attributable profit RM42.9M vs RM7.7M YoY; revenue RM457.0M vs RM369.8M YoY.

names.thesis: PT Timah's Q1 2026 refined tin production surged 81.9% YoY to 5,630 tonnes, marking a major operational turnaround after 2024-2025 disruptions from an illegal-mining crackdown and corruption-related scrutiny, with Indonesian tin exports now normalizing (as of May 2026) and management explicitly pivoting focus from volume to profitability. BMI expects tin prices to stay elevated through 2026 on continued supply tightness (Myanmar, Indonesia), though flags possible H2 moderation as supply issues ease -- overall confirming the tin-long thesis with a credible recovery narrative, tempered by a note of caution on the back half.
catalysts: Continued production recovery toward pre-crackdown levels
New 2026-2028 labor/collective agreement reducing labor disruption risk
Management's explicit pivot to profitability-focused strategy
risks: BMI flags possible H2 2026 tin price moderation as supply issues ease
Execution risk in translating production recovery into sustained profitability
Indonesian regulatory/mining policy risk given recent corruption scrutiny

analyst.BMI: Expects elevated tin prices through 2026 with possible H2 moderation

evidence.note: Q1 2026 refined tin production +81.9% YoY to 5,630 tonnes; Indonesian exports normalizing as of May 2026 after 2024-2025 crackdown-driven declines; BMI sees elevated 2026 prices with possible H2 moderation.
New 2026-2028 collective labor agreement signed; management pivoting focus from production volume to profitability.
